content.thinking:
Good. Now I understand the setup:
- mongo-rs-0 is the primary, mongo-rs-1 and mongo-rs-2 are secondaries
- The pods run `mongod --replSet rs0 --bind_ip_all` (no TLS)
- There's an openssl-toolbox pod available
- I need to:
  1. Generate certificates using the openssl-toolbox pod
  2. Mount them into mongo-rs pods at /etc/tls
  3. Reconfigure MongoDB to require TLS

The plan:
1. Generate CA and server/client certificates in the openssl-toolbox pod
2. Create a Kubernetes Secret to store the certificates
3. Update the StatefulSet to mount that secret at /etc/tls
4. Modify the mongod command to enable TLS

For the certificate generation, I'll need to create a CA key and certificate, then generate server certificates with SANs for all the mongo-rs pod hostnames, and client certificates for authentication.

Once the secret is created and mounted, I'll add the TLS flags to mongod to require TLS connections and point to the certificate files. After the StatefulSet rolls out, I can verify that TLS connections work and non-TLS connections are rejected.

For the certificates themselves, I need to generate a server cert that covers all three replica set pod hostnames plus the service DNS name so any pod can authenticate as the server. Since MongoDB replica set members also communicate over TLS when in requireTLS mode, a single cert with multiple Subject Alternative Names is the simplest approach rather than managing individual certs per node. is valid for client connections. For inter-node communication in the replica set, I can use `--tlsAllowConnectionsWithoutCertificates` so members can connect to each other over TLS without requiring client certificates, while still allowing the client.pem to authenticate external connections. With `--tlsAllowConnectionsWithoutCertificates`, clients can connect without presenting a certificate as long as they use TLS encryption, which means the replica set members can authenticate to each other through the replica set name alone without needing x509 or keyFile setup. The real constraint is that unencrypted connections get rejected, so I need to verify whether the replica set members can actually communicate when there's no explicit internal authentication mechanism configured.

For the server certificate, I need to make sure the SAN includes all the hostnames the replica set members will use to connect to each other—the Kubernetes DNS names for each pod in the stateful set. Writing certificate generation commands... Finishing the client certificate generation...

Now I need to create a Kubernetes Secret with these certificates and update the StatefulSet to use them, then write this all out as an executable script.

content.text:
Good. The replica set is healthy with a PRIMARY and 2 SECONDARIEs. Now I'll generate certificates in the openssl-toolbox pod, create a Secret, then reconfigure the StatefulSet to require TLS.

**Step 1: Generate CA, server, and client certificates**

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- bash -c '
cd /tmp

# Generate CA
openssl genrsa -out ca.key 4096 2>/dev/null
openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key ca.key -sha256 -days 3650 -out ca.crt \
  -subj "/CN=MongoDB-CA/O=MongoDB" 2>/dev/null

# Generate server key + CSR with SANs for all replica set members
openssl genrsa -out server.key 4096 2>/dev/null

cat > /tmp/server.cnf << "ENDCNF"
[req]
req_extensions = v3_req
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
[req_distinguished_name]
[v3_req]
subjectAltName = @alt_names
[alt_names]
DNS.1 = localhost
DNS.2 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.3 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.4 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.6 = mongo-rs-0.mongo
DNS.7 = mongo-rs-1.mongo
DNS.8 = mongo-rs-2.mongo
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
ENDCNF

openssl req -new -key server.key -out server.csr \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-rs/O=MongoDB" \
  -config /tmp/server.cnf 2>/dev/null

openssl x509 -req -in server.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out server.crt -days 3650 -sha256 \
  -extensions v3_req -extfile /tmp/server.cnf 2>/dev/null

cat server.crt server.key > server.pem

# Generate client cert
openssl genrsa -out client.key 4096 2>/dev/null
openssl req -new -key client.key -out client.csr \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-client/O=MongoDB" 2>/dev/null
openssl x509 -req -in client.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out client.crt -days 3650 -sha256 2>/dev/null
cat client.crt client.key > client.pem

echo "=== Done ==="
ls -la /tmp/{ca.crt,server.pem,client.pem}
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt server.crt
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt client.crt
'
input.description: Generate CA, server, and client certificates in openssl-toolbox pod

input.command:
kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
cd /tmp

# Generate CA
openssl genrsa -out ca.key 4096 2>/dev/null
openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key ca.key -sha256 -days 3650 -out ca.crt \
  -subj "/CN=MongoDB-CA/O=MongoDB" 2>/dev/null
echo "CA generated"

# Server key
openssl genrsa -out server.key 4096 2>/dev/null

# Write CNF for SANs
printf "[req]\nreq_extensions = v3_req\ndistinguished_name = req_distinguished_name\n[req_distinguished_name]\n[v3_req]\nsubjectAltName = @alt_names\n[alt_names]\nDNS.1 = localhost\nDNS.2 = mongo-rs-0.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local\nDNS.3 = mongo-rs-1.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local\nDNS.4 = mongo-rs-2.mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local\nDNS.5 = mongo.mongodb.svc.cluster.local\nDNS.6 = mongo-rs-0.mongo\nDNS.7 = mongo-rs-1.mongo\nDNS.8 = mongo-rs-2.mongo\nIP.1 = 127.0.0.1\n" > /tmp/server.cnf

openssl req -new -key server.key -out server.csr \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-rs/O=MongoDB" \
  -config /tmp/server.cnf 2>/dev/null

openssl x509 -req -in server.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out server.crt -days 3650 -sha256 \
  -extensions v3_req -extfile /tmp/server.cnf 2>/dev/null
cat server.crt server.key > server.pem
echo "Server cert generated"

# Client cert
openssl genrsa -out client.key 4096 2>/dev/null
openssl req -new -key client.key -out client.csr \
  -subj "/CN=mongo-client/O=MongoDB" 2>/dev/null
openssl x509 -req -in client.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out client.crt -days 3650 -sha256 2>/dev/null
cat client.crt client.key > client.pem
echo "Client cert generated"

ls -la /tmp/ca.crt /tmp/server.pem /tmp/client.pem
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt server.crt
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt client.crt
'

content.text: **Step 2: Extract certs and create a Kubernetes Secret**

input.command:
CA_CRT=$(kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c 'cat /tmp/ca.crt' | base64 -w0)
SERVER_PEM=$(kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c 'cat /tmp/server.pem' | base64 -w0)
CLIENT_PEM=$(kubectl exec -n mongodb openssl-toolbox -- sh -c 'cat /tmp/client.pem' | base64 -w0)

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: mongo-tls
  namespace: mongodb
type: Opaque
data:
  ca.crt: ${CA_CRT}
  server.pem: ${SERVER_PEM}
  client.pem: ${CLIENT_PEM}
EOF
input.description: Create Kubernetes Secret with TLS certificates

content.text: **Step 3: Patch the StatefulSet to mount TLS certs and enable requireTLS**
